_doc: BKDS Agentic OS — 282 hypothetical vision stories across 4 epics. These are thought provokers and conversation starters for stakeholder engagement, not a literal project plan. Each story is a starting point for iterative refinement with caregivers, clinicians, and domain experts.

_personas.end_user: person with profound autism using the kiosk interface
_personas.caretaker: primary daily caregiver (family member or DSP)
_personas.guardian: legal guardian / family member with rights/policy questions
_personas.case_manager: service coordinator managing waiver / IEP transition
_personas.system: background platform service or automated agent

stories.s: As the caretaker, I want detailed interaction analytics with clear visualizations (like heatmaps and word clouds), so that I can understand the end user's engagement and communication patterns.

As the caretaker, I want clear visualizations (such as word clouds) of the most frequently used words from the end user's inputs, so I can gain rapid insight into their current interests and communication patterns.
As the caretaker, I want complex search phrases to be intelligently summarized into concise forms, so it's easier to quickly grasp core interests without unnecessary detail.
As the caretaker, I want the ability to "drill down" from summarized search phrases to see the complete original query or voice command, so I can understand full context and nuances when necessary.
As the caretaker, I want detailed insights into the end user's interaction patterns by time of day, so I can understand when they are most active or might struggle.

As the caretaker, I want future capabilities clearly outlined to track and visualize the types of devices the end user interacts with over time, enabling future multi-device support or understanding potential device-specific challenges.

As the caretaker, I want clear tracking of end user engagement interactions ("likes," "favorites," "saves") within the app, summarized visually and sortable, so I can understand what content specifically resonates with them.

As the caretaker, I want easy-to-understand visualizations or reports summarizing the frequency and type of UI interactions (touch, keyboard, voice command) alongside engagement metrics, so I can optimize the interface and user experience.
As the caretaker, I want tracking for engagement metrics (likes, favorites, saves), so that I can understand what content resonates most with the end user.
As the caretaker, I want regular summary reports on the end user's progress and independence, so that I can monitor their development and adjust support as needed.
As the caretaker, I want clear, visually summarized user satisfaction and engagement metrics, so that I can understand the end user's emotional state and enjoyment of the application.

As an administrator or caretaker, I want analytical dashboards or integration points to visualize usage patterns, search trends, and interaction effectiveness so that I can optimize user engagement and application performance.

As an administrator or caretaker, I want to view a dashboard that visualizes engagement analytics for generated content—highlighting the most and least popular subjects, categories, and items—so that I can gain insight into the user's interests and the overall performance of the feature.
As a provider, I want to opt-in to a secure, vetted, and moderated forum, so I can collaborate with other professionals.
As a guardian, I want to join a moderated forum to discuss state waiver navigation and share AAC strategies, so I can learn from my peers.
As a researcher, I want to engage the community to gather feedback and co-design new features, so we can accelerate the R&D cycle.

As an administrator or caretaker, I want to securely configure the connection settings (e.g., IMAP server, port, username, password) for a user's email account so the system can access their messages.
As an administrator or caretaker, I want to manage a list of approved email senders within the user's profile so that the system only ingests and processes messages from these trusted contacts.
As an administrator or caretaker, I want to securely configure the connection to a phone number or SMS gateway service so the system can access messages.
As an administrator or caretaker, I want to manage a list of approved phone numbers in the user's profile so that the system only ingests messages from these trusted contacts.
As a caretaker, if an email or SMS is flagged by the content filter, I want it placed in a separate queue for my review, preventing it from being displayed to the user.
As an administrator or caretaker, I want to configure the notification behavior for new messages (e.g., visual-only, subtle sound alert) to ensure it is not overly distracting for the user.

As a caretaker who needs to protect a user I need a filter mechanism for voice searches to ensure profanity or explicit terms can be filtered and that I can have visibility of the control level. Caretaker preference is default STRICT filter. This filtering should happen in real time after utterance is detected to be ended or user has paused speaking sufficiently.
As the caretaker, I want robust content filtering and Browse safety controls, so that I can ensure the end user accesses appropriate and safe content.

As the caretaker, I want all web access to be protected by layered controls, ranging from standard Google Family-type account controls to custom high-security integrations, so I can choose the right level of safety.
As an administrator, I want to be able to configure the maximum number of YouTube videos (X), Flickr images (N), and Wikimedia Commons images (N) to be fetched for media enrichment.

As an administrator, I want to be able to configure the time interval and sensitivity for de-duplicating the user's search feed so that I can balance feed cleanliness with system performance and user experience.

As an administrator or caretaker, I want to be able to enable or disable the proactive content library feature on a per-user basis and configure parameters such as the frequency of generation and content depth.

As an administrator, I want to configure the parameters for the Content Expansion Governor, including the relative expansion factor (e.g., 10x) and the absolute maximum number of new categories (e.g., 200), so that I can balance content richness with processing costs and time.

As an administrator, I want to configure the parameters for the Prompt Generation Governor (PCL_022), such as the maximum number of prompts to generate per category, so that I can fine-tune the balance between content variety and processing workload.
As an administrator or caretaker, I want an interface to review and approve or reject stories from the 'pending approval' queue to ensure the factual accuracy and appropriateness of the content.

As a caretaker, I want a simple interface, accessible from the user's profile, that allows me to view the high-level Subjects (e.g., "Military Aviation," "Wild Animals") that the system has generated from the user's interests.

As a caretaker, when viewing the list of generated Subjects, I want to be able to select one and see the associated Categories (e.g., "Famous Fighter Jets") and their meta_category tags so I can understand how the system is breaking down a subject.

As an administrator, I want to define and manage a library of 'Prompt Angles' or 'Probing Styles' (e.g., 'Top 10 Most Common...', 'List 8 Key Features of...') that the system can use during prompt generation (PCL_021) to ensure content variety.
As an administrator, I want the ability to view, add, and edit the prompt templates in the prompt library so that I can manually refine and expand the system's creative capabilities.
As the caretaker, I want a multi-user profile management interface, so that I can easily switch between and update settings for multiple end users.
As the caretaker, I want a journaling (text or voice input) interface to record observations, so that the agent can adapt its interactions based on my input.
As the caretaker, I want automatic suggestions for speech and interaction rules, so that I can review and apply them to improve the end user's experience.
As the caretaker, I want a simple way to enter user preferences and personality insights, so that the agent can personalize interactions.

As a caretaker (or user), I want to interact with a chat interface (text or voice) to create, update, and manage the application user's profile so that their specific needs and preferences are captured.
As a caretaker (or user), I want the chat agent to guide me through an initial profile setup wizard to capture essential information.

As a caretaker (or user), during the initial setup, I want to be prompted for and capture information for predefined categories: Favorite Things, Favorite Places, Favorite Food, Favorite Activity, Repetitive Spurious Phrases, Common Nonsense Words/Noises, and Fears/Anxieties (if applicable).

As a caretaker (or user), I want to provide free-form text or voice input regarding the user's characteristics, and I want the chat agent to intelligently categorize and store this information in the appropriate profile section.

As a caretaker (or user), after providing new profile information or updates, I want the chat agent to confirm the changes and display the updated profile characteristics in a clear and understandable way (e.g., a summary of changes, or the relevant updated section).
As a caretaker (or user), I want to be able to request a full display of the user's current profile, with options to view it in a conversational chat style or as structured JSON data.

As a caretaker (or user), I want to be able to ask the chat agent questions about the currently stored user profile characteristics (e.g., "What are their favorite foods?", "Are there any fears listed?") and receive accurate answers.
As a caretaker (or user), I want to be able to easily edit or delete existing profile information through the chat interface.
As a caretaker or administrator, I want version control and snapshot capabilities for user profiles so that I can easily revert or compare profile changes over time.
As an administrator (or therapist), I want to be able to manage and update the user-specific word bank for spurious words so that the hygiene process remains effective for each user.
As a provider, I want to search for I/DD communication support, AAC suggestions, and environmental adaptations, so I can find best practices quickly.
As a guardian, I want to read plain-language explanations of federal regulations like ICF/IID and HCBS person-centered planning, so I can understand my rights.
As a case manager, I want to access a 'State Program Pack' for my state, so I can find details on state-specific waivers, eligibility, and EVV rules.
As a guardian, I want a one-click guide on how to appeal a managed care (42 CFR 438) decision, so I can navigate the state fair hearing process.
As an agency administrator, I want a centralized dashboard that visualizes key metrics like PCP/ISP goal attainment and incident report frequency, so I can manage state reporting and internal QA.
As a DSP, I want to review my individual's daily PCP/ISP goals on the appliance when I start my shift, so I know what to focus on.
As a DSP, I want to log an activity outcome ('what was offered/accepted') in under 30 seconds using a simple interface, so I can spend more time on care.
As a case manager, I want logged outcomes from the Individual Application to be automatically formatted for monthly reports, so I can reduce administrative time.

As the caretaker, I want to create a structured 'Individual Traits' profile based on my knowledge of the user's needs, triggers, communication mode, and specific interests (e.g., 'Loves aviation, San Diego Zoo').
As the caretaker, I want all 'Individual Traits' to be versioned with a consent history, so I have a clear audit trail of what was enabled and when.
As the end user, I want the agent to provide direct AAC support, like generating symbol libraries and visual schedules, so I can communicate effectively.
As the end user, I want the ADLs Helper to assist me with daily tasks and reminders, based on my person-centered plan (PCP/ISP).

As the caretaker, I want the ADLs Helper to be programmed with 'Dignity of Risk' and 'Skill Acquisition Logic', so it periodically introduces novel, adjacent skills (within safety parameters) to promote growth, not just maintenance.
As the end user, I want the AI to curate and bank stories based on my interests, so I can explore things I love.
As the caretaker, I want the agent to gracefully guide the user to new content, so it can help break obsessive loops (OCD/intense interests) by finding related topics within parameters I set.
As the agent, I want to regularly and securely process local browser and voice history data, so that I can personalize content and interactions for the end user.
As the caretaker, I want the agent to periodically collect and analyze the end user's browser history and voice search data, ensuring updated insights without interrupting their current interactions.
As the caretaker, I want to configure sensible rules about how frequently browser and voice search histories are harvested, balancing the need for accurate information with system performance.
As the caretaker, I want clear summaries or visual indicators showing trends or notable shifts in end user interests derived from browser and voice search histories, enabling proactive adjustments.

As the end user, when the agent updates its understanding based on my history, I want a subtle but clear visual or auditory cue that personalized content has been refreshed or improved, reinforcing my engagement. These notification level can be configured by caretaker.
As the agent, I want to proactively simplify the interface based on periodic screenshot analysis, so that I can adapt to the end user's interaction patterns and reduce complexity.
As the caretaker, I want the processing of screenshot data to occur during times of low end user activity, preventing any noticeable impact on system performance or responsiveness.

As the caretaker, I want clear guidelines and easily adjustable settings regarding how long historical Browse, voice search, and screenshot data are retained, balancing personalization benefits with privacy and storage.

As the end user, I want visual and auditory cues when personalized content has been updated, so that I know the agent is learning and adapting to me. These notification level can be configured by caretaker.

As the end user, I want proactive real-time support when I encounter difficulty, so that I can overcome challenges and continue my tasks without frustration. These notification level can be configured by caretaker.
As the caretaker, I want to receive timely notifications when analysis of end user interaction patterns suggests significant ongoing frustration or difficulty, enabling me to intervene directly.

As the caretaker, I want the agent to identify patterns from screenshot data that indicate repetitive UI missteps, automatically suggesting adjustments to simplify or streamline the end user's environment.
As the caretaker, I want clear guidelines for data retention, so that I can balance personalization benefits with privacy, security, and storage considerations.
As the caretaker, I want clear guidelines for data retention, so that I can balance personalization benefits with privacy, security, and storage considerations.
As the end user, I want a custom, highly controlled interface for messaging, calls, and photo sharing, so I can connect with my family without the complexity of traditional social media.
As the caretaker, I want to manage a 'trusted list' of friends and family who can contact the end user, so I can prevent all spam and scams.
As a trusted family member, I want a simple web or mobile portal to send a photo or message to the end user, so I can safely share my life with them.
As the caretaker, I want to receive notifications and optionally review messages/photos sent to the end user, so I can ensure their safety and well-being.
As a caretaker, if an email or SMS is flagged by the content filter, I want it placed in a separate queue for my review, preventing it from being displayed to the user.
As a user, I want to see new email messages appear in my primary content feed, formatted as a standard story and clearly identifying the sender.
As a user, when viewing a message, I want to see the summarized content by default, with an option to easily view the original, full email body.
As a user, I want a clear and simple visual indicator on the main screen (e.g., a mail icon with a number) to know how many new messages are available for me to view.
As a user, I want to find all messages from a specific person with no more than two clicks from the main feed so that I can easily review past conversations.
As a user, I want to see new SMS/MMS messages appear in my primary content feed, formatted as a standard story and clearly identifying the sender by name or number.
As a trusted family member, I want a simple web or mobile portal to send a photo or message to the end user, so I can safely share my life with them.
As a user, I want to see the results of my query clearly displayed in the UI so that I can understand the information provided.

As a user, when the query result is determined to be suitable for rich content (e.g., a major landmark), I want to see it in a "Default Format" that includes sections for descriptions, LLM-provided media, additionally fetched YouTube videos, Flickr images, Wikimedia images, and related links.

As a user, when the query result is identified as a specific local address or business, I want to see it in a "Place Page" format, which prioritizes a map view, address, contact information, and hours.
As a user, I want to see system-generated library content in a distinct "Library Story" format, ensuring the content is presented in an engaging, article-like manner.
As a user, I want intuitive interactions to expand or zoom into images and videos from the default result view so that I can easily explore media content.

As a user, I want my primary feed to display a blended, chronological view of both my personal voice search results and the system-generated library content so that I have a single place for discovery and review.
As a user or caretaker, I want the ability to filter my feed to show only my search history, only the system-generated library, or both, so that I can easily navigate the content.
As a user, I want to see a chronological feed of my past voice searches and their results displayed in the UI so that I can easily review my search history.

As a user, when viewing my search feed, I want to be able to select a past search to quickly re-display its full results (including all associated media) so that I can easily access previous information.
As a user or caretaker, I want intelligent filtering options in the search history feed (such as date, content type, keywords) so that I can quickly locate past searches.
As the end user, I want a voice-centric LLM interface by default, with options for an online keyboard or an online symbol board, so I can communicate in the way that is best for me.
As the end user, I want to activate voice input with a simple action (e.g., button press, voice command) so that I can easily start a search.
As the end user, I want the system to capture my speech accurately via the microphone so that my intended query is understood.
As the end user, I want to see a visual indication that the system is listening and processing my speech so that I know it's working.
As the end user, I want to be able to stop or cancel the voice input easily so that I can correct mistakes or change my mind or retry.
As the end user, I want my voice searches to intelligently detect when I am finished speaking.
As the caretaker, I want voice searches to automatically submit or expire based on a configurable timeout, so the user can choose to manually intervene or have them submit instantaneously.
As the end user, I want immediate, minimal visual and auditory feedback on the accuracy of my voice recognition (like a confidence meter), so that I know if my query was clearly understood.
As the caretaker, I want to configure thresholds for the agent to prompt for clarification when speech clarity is low, so that the agent adapts to the end user's individual speech peculiarities.
As the end user, I want clearly labeled programmable buttons for functions like Help, Yes/No, and Repeat, so that I can easily perform basic actions.
As the end user, I want to control the pacing of the agent's spoken output, so that I can comprehend information at my own speed.

As a user, when I make a query that I've made recently, I want to receive the results (including all associated media) quickly by bypassing the LLM API call and additional media fetching if a valid cached result exists, so that I save time.
As the caretaker, I want to configure usage limits and gentle reminders for breaks, so that I can promote healthy screen time habits for the end user.
As the caretaker, I want an optional, configurable anxiety management or calming module, so that the agent can help the end user de-escalate frustration and promote calm.
As the end user, I want real-time recovery from unexpected application or interface disruptions, so that I can continue my activities without frustration or needing assistance.

As the end user, I want an adaptive calming response from the agent when I show signs of frustration, so that I can regulate my emotions and continue my interaction. These notification level can be configured by caretaker.
As the caretaker, I want controllable alerts on user frustration patterns, so that I can intervene promptly if needed. These notification level can be configured by caretaker.

As the end user, I want proactive clarification prompts from the agent during interaction confusion, so that I can resolve misunderstandings and continue my tasks smoothly. The caretaker can always control proactive remediation levels. These notification level can be configured by caretaker.
As the end user, I want a clear 'ask my caregiver' route surfaced when I am making a complex decision, so I can get help when I need it.
As a user with echolalia, I want the system to identify and filter out my repeated words or phrases based on my personal word bank so that my search query is more accurate and less cluttered.

As a user with palilalia, I want the system to detect and reduce my own involuntarily repeated phrases (e.g., "yellow rose of texas, yellow rose, like a yellow rose of texas" becomes "yellow rose of texas") so that the core intent of my speech is captured.
As a user who tends towards circumstantial speech, I want the system to condense my detailed descriptions into focused search terms or keywords so that the LLM receives a concise and relevant query.
As the end user, I want the system to filter repetitive speech in real-time, so that my queries are accurate and focused even if I exhibit echolalia or repetitive speech patterns.
As the caretaker, I want to configure rules for filtering the end user's speech, so that I can adapt the system to their unique speech peculiarities.

As the caretaker, I want to customize the speech filtering to the known characteristics of my end user (e.g., specific repeated phrases, fixations, perseveration tendencies etc.) so the agent is tuned for this end user's tendencies and traits.

As the end user, I want the application to automatically launch into a simplified, full-screen kiosk mode upon boot-up, so that it is the primary and only visible interface and I can immediately start using it.
As the caretaker, I want a secure, hidden method to exit or unlock the application, so that the end user has a stable and predictable experience without accidentally leaving the app.
As the end user, I want the application to automatically recover from crashes in a robust way, so that I can continue using it without interruption or needing assistance.

As the end user, I want an easy to find set of options to sleep, restart, or power off the PC. The options should speak to the user what is happening in a calm quite voice and play a chime specific to the option.
As the end user, I want short textual summaries presented in large, clear fonts with generous spacing, enhancing quick comprehension.
As the end user, I want key terms subtly highlighted during summaries, supporting comprehension without distraction.
As the end user, I want an easy-to-find, single-action "Undo" button prominently displayed.
As the end user, I want a streamlined visual query builder with clear icons and step-by-step guidance.
As the end user, I want simplified, clearly visualized access to my past searches displayed in minimalistic cards.
As the caretaker, I want to configure summarization rules for search histories, ensuring clarity.
As the end user, I want an intuitive "Save Favorites" icon clearly positioned within reach.
As the end user, I want personalized content clearly labeled as "Agent's Pick," prominently placed.
As the end user, when my queries are ambiguous, I want proactive, simple clarification prompts ensuring accurate results.
As the end user, after a successful search, I want minimalistic visual suggestions for related topics.
As the end user, I want a prominently displayed "Home" button allowing immediate return to the initial interface.
As the end user, I want guaranteed rapid result loading (under 2 seconds latency), so that I remain engaged and don't get frustrated by delays.
As the end user, I want consistent, simplified high-contrast layouts, so that the interface is easy to comprehend and interact with.
As the end user, I want to control the auditory pacing and content playback, so that I can process information at my own comfortable speed.

As a user with accessibility needs, I want the UI to support assistive technologies (such as screen readers, ARIA labels), adjustable text sizes, and high-contrast modes so that I can comfortably and independently access information.
As the end user, I want the UI to update efficiently when new results, including any additionally fetched media, are available so that I don't experience unnecessary delays.
As the end user, I want to be able to easily clear the current results or start a new search so that I can continue interacting with the application.
As the end user, I want the UI layout to adapt automatically to different device types (kiosk, desktop, tablet, mobile) so that I have an optimized experience regardless of the device I use.
As the end user, I want to be able to easily clear the current results or start a new search so that I can continue interacting with the application.
As the end user, I want the UI layout to adapt automatically to different device types (kiosk, desktop, tablet, mobile) so that I have an optimized experience regardless of the device I use.
As the end user, I want an immediate and minimal summary (audio and text) of my search topic, clearly emphasizing key points, so I quickly understand the core information without distractions.

As the end user, I want a clear, prominently placed "Tell Me More" button that incrementally reveals additional details in manageable chunks, so I can comfortably explore deeper information without feeling overwhelmed.

As the end user, I want Wikipedia/Wikimedia content presented with no more than three clearly labeled simplified summaries and minimal imagery per query, so I can easily select and comprehend my topic of interest.

As the end user, I want a clear, single-screen interactive grid layout displaying up to 12 curated topical images from Flickr and Google Images per query, so I can visually explore without becoming distracted. Include a configurable "Get More" option with API-usage guardrails.
As the end user, I want Google Maps/Earth location previews clearly displayed with one primary image and minimal descriptive text per location, ensuring quick visual understanding of places.
As the end user, I want a single-click button clearly labeled "Explore in Google Maps/Earth" that smoothly transitions me to the full app when desired, ensuring a seamless exploration experience.
As the end user, I want concise YouTube video previews limited to a single still-frame thumbnail and a brief, simplified text description, so I can quickly identify content without clutter.

As the end user, I want seamless, instantaneous playback transitions between preview and full video either within the application or directly opening the YouTube app, ensuring my viewing experience remains smooth and intuitive.

As the end user, I want the ability to intuitively transition from the simplified interface to either the full YouTube web app or a custom limited-view YouTube interface, ensuring comfortable and accessible content exploration.
As the end user, I want a maximum of three simplified Wikipedia results per query, ensuring manageable choices.
As the end user, I want image sets from Wikimedia and Flickr limited to six items per query, ensuring visual engagement without cognitive overload.
As the end user, I want Google Images displayed in a simplified single-screen layout with minimal scrolling, limited to six visuals.
As the end user, I want simplified location listings (maximum three) for Google Maps/Earth, ensuring easy choices.
As the end user, I want limited, simplified YouTube thumbnails (maximum four) with concise descriptions.

As the end user, when I request information about a location, I want to see a simplified, interactive map display (resembling Google Earth's globe view or Google Maps' satellite view) with clear geographical landmarks, so that I can visually understand the location without unnecessary details.

As the end user, I want to be able to zoom in/out, pan, and tilt the map view using simple, large, clearly labeled on-screen controls or pre-programmed physical buttons/voice commands, so that I can explore the geospatial content without accidentally altering any data or settings.

As the end user, I want the map display to actively avoid showing commercial points of interest (like shops or restaurants) or excessive labels, unless specifically requested, so that I can concentrate purely on geographical features without distraction or irrelevant commercial content.

As the end user, I want the agent to offer simple, optional toggles (e.g., "Show Photos," "Show Roads") that are clearly labeled and appear by default, so that I can choose to add basic contextual information to the map view if I desire, without complex layers.

As the end user, I want the map interface to offer only essential viewing and navigation controls, explicitly avoiding options for "creating projects," "sharing," "measuring distances," or complex routing, so that I remain focused on exploration and avoid confusion from advanced features.

As the end user, when viewing a custom map, I want a prominent "Return to Agent" button (large, consistent placement) or a simple voice command, so that I can quickly and easily exit the map view and return to the main AI agent interface.
As the agent, I want to actively maintain boundaries by routing medical diagnosis content to clinicians and crisis content to 988, so the user is kept safe.
As the agent, I want to actively maintain boundaries by routing medical diagnosis content to clinicians and crisis content to 988, so the user is kept safe.

As the caretaker, I want all web access to be protected by layered controls, ranging from standard Google Family-type account controls to custom high-security integrations, so I can choose the right level of safety.
As a system, I want to integrate semantic tagging and categorization of media and text content using NLP models so that content retrieval and filtering become more accurate.
As a system, I want robust intent classification algorithms to identify user intent beyond basic keyword extraction so that query relevance and accuracy are significantly improved.
As a system, I want an asynchronous processing pipeline (using BullMQ) that decouples media retrieval and LLM interactions from the UI thread to enhance responsiveness.
As a system, I want to periodically (e.g., every 5 minutes) de-duplicate the user's search feed by consolidating identical or highly similar recent queries, showing only the most recent instance.
As a system, I want to intelligently merge related queries into topic clusters during the de-duplication process so that the user's feed remains clear and organized.
As an agency administrator, I want to configure the platform's FHIR API to securely connect with our existing Electronic Health Record (EHR) system, so data can be synced.
As a case manager, I want the 'goal attainment' data from the provider dashboard to securely sync with our EHR via FHIR, so I can eliminate double data entry for state reporting.
As the caretaker, I want standardized internal UI event codes assigned to UI/UX elements, so I can enable precise tracking, reporting, and analytics of end user actions across the interface.
As the caretaker, I want standardized UI event codes for precise analytics, so that I can gain deep insights into the end user's interactions and system performance.

As a system, I want to establish a decoupled service architecture where core functions (e.g., user profiles, logging) are exposed through secure, internal APIs so that new, independent feature modules can be developed and integrated without modifying the core system.

As a system, I want to define a standardized API and data contract for new modules so that any future feature (e.g., voice calling, dietary guide) can be integrated predictably by adhering to a clear and versioned interface.

As a system, I want the main user interface to be designed as a "shell" application capable of dynamically loading and displaying distinct front-end modules or applications from a central navigation point.

As a system, I want to evolve the UI format selection beyond 'Default vs. Place Page' logic into a data-driven registry, where any new content type can be mapped to a corresponding UI rendering component.

As a system, I want a generic framework for adding and managing connections to external, third-party APIs (e.g., Flickr Pro, health apps), which standardizes authentication, data fetching, and error handling.

As a system, I want to leverage the asynchronous processing pipeline to create pluggable stages, allowing new data sources or processing steps to be added to workflows without disrupting existing logic.

As an administrator or caretaker, I want an interface to view, enable, disable, and provide configurations for available application modules on a per-user basis so that I can safely manage and tailor the user's experience with new features.

As a system, I want to generate a unique key (e.g., hash of the condensed query) for each query-result pair (including LLM response and media references) so that I can identify identical queries for API call bypassing.
As a system, I want to store the LLM result and media references associated with its unique key in a cache (like Redis) so that I can retrieve it quickly for repetitive queries.
As a system, I want each cached key/result for API bypassing to have a configurable expiration time so that results are refreshed periodically.
As an administrator, I want to be able to configure the default expiration time for cached results (used for API bypassing) so that I can balance performance with data freshness.
As a system, when a cached result (for API bypassing) is requested but has expired, I want to re-fetch the result from the LLM API and media, update the cache, and then return the result.
As a system, I want to perform regular cache consistency checks and automated invalidation after significant profile updates or media source changes.
As a system, I want to cache thumbnail images and associated media metadata locally so that I reduce latency and improve UI rendering speed.
As a system, I want to periodically clear outdated cached media based on configurable time-to-live policies to maintain efficient storage use.
As a system, I want adaptive caching strategies (such as LRU or frequency-based caching) to manage cache efficiently based on query usage patterns.
As a system, I need to implement a strategy for caching the intermediate outputs of the content generation workflow (e.g., category breakdowns and item lists) to manage API costs and reduce latency.

As a system, upon detecting a meaningful change in the user's profile interests ("Favorite Things," "Favorite Places"), I want to automatically trigger the Content Index Generation process on a flexible schedule (manual, on change, or scheduled interval).

As a system, I want to perform an initial analysis of the user's profile to identify broad subjects, decompose them into diverse categories, and generate metadata, ensuring meta_category tags are from the admin-defined taxonomy.

As a system, after the initial index generation, I want to perform a single, secondary pass that treats newly generated metaKeywords as new subjects to discover and generate additional, more specific categories.

As a system, during the keyword-driven expansion loop (PCL_012), I want to apply a governor that limits the creation of new categories by adhering to a configurable relative expansion factor and an absolute maximum number.
As a system, during the keyword-driven expansion loop, I want to check for and discard any newly generated subjects or categories that are duplicates of those already present.
As a system, after the initial generation and expansion loop are complete, I want to persistently store the final, enriched Content Index JSON artifact for each user.
As a system, I want to process a stored Content Index and generate a comprehensive set of list-prompts by targeting multiple levels of granularity: Subject, Category, and Category+metaKeywords.

As a system, for each topic targeted by the prompt generator, I want to create a small, diverse set of prompts that explore the topic from different "Prompt Angles" (e.g., 'most common examples,' 'rarest examples').
As a system, during the multi-level prompt generation process, I want to apply a governor to limit the total number of varied prompts created per Category to a configurable maximum.
As a system, I want to asynchronously execute the entire set of LLM-generated prompts to produce structured lists of items.

As a system, for each individual item on a generated list, I want to perform a subsequent LLM inquiry to generate a rich, descriptive "story" or summary about that item using a fixed template incorporating user profile data.
As a system, I want to store the fully generated story content in a structured format, marking it clearly as system-generated library content.
As a system, I want the entire content library generation process (from indexing to story creation) to run as a low-priority, asynchronous background task using a message queue (like BullMQ).
As a system, during the Content Index generation process, I want to perform a quality check on the output to detect issues like high redundancy or excessive generality.
As a system, if the automated quality check (PCL_080) fails on the generated categories, I want to re-initiate the decomposition prompt with modified instructions to attempt to self-correct.
As a system, I want to place all newly generated stories into a 'pending approval' state and present them in a dedicated administrative queue so they are not visible to the end-user until validated.
As a system, I want to implement configurable retry logic for each individual LLM call within the content generation workflow so that transient network or API errors can be automatically handled.
As a system, if a step in the content generation workflow ultimately fails after all retries, I want to log the specific failure and gracefully proceed to the next item or category.
As a system, if a step in the content generation workflow ultimately fails after all retries, I want to log the specific failure and gracefully proceed to the next item or category.
As a product owner, I want all PII-touching features to be audited for HIPAA and 42 CFR Part 2 (SUD) compliance, so we maintain regulatory standards.
As a researcher, I want to manage an opt-in, de-identified data program governed by the Data Steward, so we can ethically improve the product.
As a designer, I want to co-design new features with end users and caretakers, so we build what is actually needed and meet accessibility goals.

As a system, I want to persistently store each complete result set (including the condensed query, the LLM response, any additionally fetched media, and the display format decision) so that it can be quickly retrieved.
As a system, I need to securely manage user profiles and associated data.
As a system (chat agent), I want to securely store and retrieve all user profile information.
As a system administrator, I want to implement structured storage solutions (e.g., PostgreSQL or SQLite) to efficiently store and query user profiles, cached responses, search history, and metadata.
As a Data Steward, I want a 'Governance console' to manage the 'Trusted Source allow-list' (preferring .gov domains), so I can ensure all knowledge bank and web access content is vetted.

As a product owner, I want to submit AI 'best-practice rails' and 'Skill Acquisition Logic' to the external Clinical & Ethical Review Board for periodic audit, so we can prevent algorithmic bias and align with clinical standards.

As an administrator, I want to define and manage a system-wide taxonomy of valid meta_category tags (e.g., Person, Place, Thing, Animal, Activity) so that I can ensure all generated content is consistently and accurately classified according to a controlled vocabulary.

As an administrator, I want to maintain and utilize a library of multiple, varied prompt templates for each stage of the content generation workflow so that I can rotate or randomly select them to ensure the style and structure of generated content remain diverse over time.
As a system, I want to securely encrypt and store all email account credentials to protect user data and privacy.
As a system, I want to run the email checking and processing functions as a recurring, asynchronous background task to ensure the user interface remains responsive.
As a system, I want to periodically connect to the configured email account, fetch new messages, and filter them to only process those from the predefined list of approved senders.
As a system, I want to screen the subject and body of each fetched email for adult, profane, or explicit content based on configurable rules to ensure user safety.

As a system, for each approved email, I want to use an LLM to generate a single, high-level summary sentence that describes the email's overall content (e.g., "Jane sends an update about the party with pictures").
As a system, for each approved email, I want to use an LLM to generate a brief summary paragraph of the email's body.
As a system, I want to detect, extract, and process any image attachments from an email so they can be displayed properly in the UI.
As a system, I want to detect, extract, and process any video attachments from an email so they can be played in the UI.
As a system, I want to store the processed message—including summaries, original body, sender, and attachments—using the default story content format for standardized UI rendering.
As a system, I want to periodically check for new SMS/MMS messages from the user's predefined list of approved phone numbers.
As a system, for each approved message, I want to use an LLM to generate a concise summary paragraph of the message content.
As a system, I want to detect and extract any image or video attachments from an MMS message so they can be displayed properly in the UI.
As a system, I want to convert the captured audio into raw text so that it can be further processed.
As a system, I want to access a user-specific word bank of spurious words so that I can effectively filter them during speech hygiene processing.
As a system, I want to identify and consolidate semantically similar repetitive phrases so that query intent is preserved while reducing redundancy.
As a system, I want to apply natural language processing techniques to extract key subjects and keywords from overly detailed speech so that a concise query can be formed.

As a system, I want to send the condensed/normalized speech-to-text content to a designated LLM agent API so that I can leverage its capabilities for generating a response (which may include some initial media content).
As a system, I want to handle API requests asynchronously so that the user interface remains responsive while waiting for the LLM.

As a system, I want to implement robust error handling for LLM API calls (e.g., timeouts, network issues, API errors) so that the application can gracefully manage failures and inform the user if necessary.
As a system, I want to receive and parse the results from the LLM API so that the information can be prepared for display.

As a system, I want to analyze the LLM response (and potentially the original query) to distinguish whether it refers to a specific local address/business or a broader landmark/topic so that the appropriate UI format can be selected.
As a system, based on the address type analysis, I want to select the "Place Page" format if the result is identified as a specific local address/business.
As a system, based on the address type analysis, I want to select the "Default Format" if the result is identified as a major landmark or general topic.
As a system (chat agent), I want to analyze incoming profile information and determine the most suitable category for storage, even if not explicitly stated by the caretaker/user.
As a system, I want to log the raw speech-to-text output for every query so that I have a record of the original user input for analysis and troubleshooting.
As a system, I want to log the condensed/normalized speech-to-text content sent to the LLM so that I can track the effectiveness of the hygiene process.
As a system, I want to log the exact request sent to the LLM API (including any parameters) so that I can debug API interactions.
As a system, I want to log the complete response received from the LLM API so that I have a record of the information provided.
As a system, I want to log details of any additional media searches performed (sources, queries, number of results fetched/attempted) for "Default Format" results.
As a system, I want to log the decision made regarding UI format selection (Default vs. Place Page) for each result, so that we can analyze the effectiveness of the distinguishing logic.
As a system, I want to generate and store metadata (e.g., keywords, topics, intent, identified address type, types and count of media fetched) based on the condensed query and the LLM result.
As an administrator (or researcher), I want to be able to access and analyze the logged data (raw speech, condensed speech, API interactions, media search details, metadata, format decisions).
As a system administrator, I want the logging infrastructure to support real-time monitoring and alerts for unusual patterns (e.g., frequent API errors, abnormal speech inputs).
As a system administrator, I want granular error logging that includes detailed context such as user profile details and preceding actions so that troubleshooting and issue resolution are expedited.
As a system administrator, I want structured logging (JSON-formatted logs) and propagation of correlation IDs to streamline data analysis, troubleshooting, and integration with monitoring systems.
As a system administrator, I want detailed performance metrics logging (such as API latency, cache hit rates, UI update timings) so that system performance can be effectively monitored and optimized.

As a system administrator, I want to record detailed logs of user interactions (e.g., voice input cancellations, profile modifications) so that I can continuously improve user experience based on actual usage patterns.

As a system, I want to log all stages of the proactive content generation process, including the subjects identified from the user profile, the prompts generated by the LLM, the lists created, and any errors encountered.
As a system, I want to log user engagement events specifically for proactively generated content, capturing metrics such as views, time spent, and any follow-up interactions.
As a system, when a query result is designated for the "Default Format", I want to initiate a secondary process to search for relevant media content from external sources to enrich the results.
As a system, I want to be able to fetch up to a configurable number (X) of relevant YouTube videos for a query designated for "Default Format".
As a system, I want to be able to fetch up to a configurable number (N) of relevant Flickr images for a query designated for "Default Format".
As a system, I want to be able to fetch up to a configurable number (N) of relevant Wikimedia Commons images for a query designated for "Default Format".
As a system, I want to handle errors gracefully if external media searches fail or return no relevant results, ensuring the primary LLM content and any successfully fetched media are still displayed.
As the caretaker, I want secure local data storage compliant with GDPR/HIPAA, so that the end user's privacy is protected and regulatory standards are met.
As the caretaker, I want comprehensive audit trails for my actions and rule changes, so that there is accountability and traceability.
As the caretaker, I want to configure data retention policies, so that I have control over how long the end user's data is stored.
As the end user, I want a clear and simple 'clear all data' function, so I have control over my information.
As an agency admin, I want to procure the Individual Application hardware via a Hardware-as-a-Service (HaaS) subscription model, so that cost, maintenance, and durability are handled by the vendor.
As an agency admin, I want to manage all our dedicated hardware appliances via a remote device management (MDM) portal, so I can handle security, software updates, and kiosk mode settings.
As an agency admin, I want a clear process for handling the replacement/refresh cycle of our HaaS devices, so I can manage budgets and logistics.
As a guardian, I want clear routing from the ASD Portal landing page into the prevalence context infographic so I can quickly find contextual data for my child's planning meetings.
As a self-advocate, I want the prevalence context infographic search to surface related topics and filters for geography and year so I can explore trends that matter to me.

As an accessibility specialist, I want the prevalence context infographic to meet WCAG 2.1 AA color contrast, keyboard navigation, and alt text expectations so the visuals are usable for all visitors.
As a product manager, I want analytics events on the prevalence context infographic that capture routing entry points, search refinements, and download interactions so I can evaluate engagement.
